text.style: font-weight: 700; font-size: 24px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans'; text-anchor: start; fill: #222831

g.text: Fig. 4.8 Pitch Coding in the Cochlea

text.style: font-size: 13.5px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans'; text-anchor: start; fill: #5f6670

g.text: Place theory and frequency theory solve different parts of the pitch problem

text.style: font-size: 12px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans'; text-anchor: middle; fill: #222831
font-size: 12px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans'; text-anchor: middle; fill: #222831
font-size: 12.5px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans'; text-anchor: middle; fill: #5f6670

g.text: Frequency theory: low pitch can also be coded by firing rate
(neurons fire in step with the sound wave, within biological limits).

text.style: font-size: 13.5px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans'; text-anchor: start; fill: #222831

g.text: Modern answer: both mechanisms are useful, but in different frequency ranges.
